I work at Sucafina with international clients.
I am currently reviewing a contract.
The shipment was confirmed last week.
Can you send the invoice, please?
Every day, I check my emails.
Right now, I am writing an email.
Last week, I confirmed the order.
Fernanda, a supplier in Switzerland sent a message.
What do they need? An update on the order?
Yes. The shipment was confirmed last week, but they want the delivery date.
Do we have the date now?
Not yet. Right now, the team is checking the documents.
Okay. I will write a short reply and confirm the date soon.
Perfect. Use a clear subject line and a polite closing.
This is my first real email in English. I am ready.

Hi Fernanda, this is Daniel from the trading team. I have a quick request for you. A supplier in Switzerland sent a message this morning. They want an update on the coffee shipment. The shipment was confirmed last week, but they need the delivery date. Right now, the logistics team is checking the documents. Please write a short email and confirm the new date. Thank you very much.

Hi, this is Fernanda. Here is my reply to the supplier. Dear partner, thank you for your message. I am writing about the coffee shipment. The order was confirmed last week. Right now, our team is checking the delivery date. We are working on it, and I will confirm the date soon. Best regards, Fernanda. I am happy because this is my first real email in English.
First, write a clear subject line. Then, start with a greeting like Dear partner. Next, write the body with your message. Finally, end with a closing like Best regards and your name.
